steps.title: Identify what the question needs
steps.content: The blank introduces the second sentence, which describes the EU creating DOP status. The first sentence establishes that rising demand for regionally labeled cheeses created a need to verify authenticity. We need a transition that signals why the EU acted: the need in sentence 1 caused the action in sentence 2.

steps.title: Eliminate the choices
steps.content:
• Choice A — Therefore,: Signals a logical consequence. Sentence 2 (EU establishes DOP) follows directly from the cause in sentence 1 (need to guarantee authenticity). This is a cause-to-effect transition. ✓
  • Choice B — Likewise,: Signals similarity or addition. But sentence 2 is not a parallel example of the same thing as sentence 1—it is the response to the problem sentence 1 describes. ✗
  • Choice C — Regardless,: Signals that sentence 2 is true despite or independent of sentence 1. That contradicts the logic: the EU acted because of the need described in sentence 1, not in spite of it. ✗
  • Choice D — Conversely,: Signals opposition or contrast. Sentence 2 does not contrast with sentence 1; it responds to it. ✗
A is correct.
